Prompt
U
Oracle Events
- Search Airtable Relocations records for all active moves with Status "In Progress" to find 27 active relocations across 8 clients (Canopy Health 5, Vectral Systems 5, BrightLoop Analytics 4, GreenStack Energy 3, Mosaic Robotics 4, NorthWind Technologies 2, PivotPoint Labs 3, StormCloud Analytics 1), with assigned coordinators, move dates, and special requirements for each.
- Search for emails related to Heartland, Jake Loomis, Swift, DOT placarding, Keiko Tanaka, Marcus Webb, and Mehta to find the Q1 billing dispute correspondence, Jake Loomis's 20% Q3 rate increase notice tying it to the unresolved dispute, Greg Pallone's confirmation that the Keiko Tanaka placarding was completed (driver Mike Becerra, Swift job SR-2026-04-1538), cold-chain arrangement details for Marcus Webb (Canopy), and Fatimah's workload on the Mehta executive relocation.
- Search Slack for Heartland, DOT, Swift, Marcus Webb, cold-chain, Fatimah, Lakeline, and coordinator workload discussions to find the billing dispute escalation, DOT audit status including Blessing's note that the signed placarding certificate has not been received, the Lakeline Logistics backup vendor option (Sandra Walsh, Midwest/Southeast corridor coverage only, not a national replacement), and coordinator capacity concerns.
- Search Linear for Operations team issues to find stale and relevant tickets including the DOT placarding subtask (issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert, state To Do, due April 23), the Heartland Q1 reconciliation ticket (issue_heartland_q1_recon, #342, overdue since April 10), the Heartland discrepancies consolidation ticket (linear_issue_6f33a369d77e, #118, due April 15), the CEO vendor strategy memo ticket (#318, due April 29, assigned to Marcus Thorne), and the Jae-won Kim reconstruction ticket (#284, assigned to Suki Patel).
- Search QuickBooks for Heartland and Swift vendor bills to find Heartland bills: HM-2026-Q1-0042 ($12,800 total, 8 moves at $1,600), HM-2026-Q1-0042-HOLD (payment hold with lines 6-8 annotated as DISPUTED), HM-2025-Q4-0031 ($6,400 fully paid), HM-2026-0418 ($2,150 outstanding for Dr. Yusuf Abdi), ACCRUAL-2026-0415-MOSAIC ($90,000 Mosaic robotic arm damage), ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001 ($50,000 contingent DOT penalty accrual). Swift bills: SR-2026-0419 (Kevin Tran $2,885), SR-2026-0421 (Priya Venkatesh $4,450), SR-2026-0422 (Ines Kovac $3,675), totaling $11,010 outstanding.
- Search CRM for Heartland Movers and Swift Relocations company records to find vendor records, relationship context, deal information, and engagement history for both vendors.
- Look up Marcus Thorne's contact details to confirm his email address marcus.thorne@moveops.com, before sending the vendor financial summary.
- Update the DOT placarding subtask (via linear_update_issue) with id issue_dot_tanaka_subtask_swift_cert from To Do to In Progress, adding a comment that Greg Pallone confirmed placarding was completed by driver Mike Becerra (Swift job SR-2026-04-1538, 49 CFR 172.504 compliant), but the signed PDF copies have not yet been received. PHMSA deadline extended to June 5, 2026.
- Update the Heartland Q1 reconciliation ticket (via linear_update_issue) with id issue_heartland_q1_recon, adding a comment that Jake Loomis has not responded to the formal dispute, instead issued a 20% Q3 rate increase notice effective July 1 and tied preferred account treatment to resolving the invoice. Hana Kim's expanded review found additional discrepancies in Kevin Tran (unauthorized storage) and Priya Nair (shuttle surcharge mismatch). This feeds into the CEO vendor strategy memo (#318, due April 29).
- Update the Heartland discrepancies consolidation ticket (via linear_update_issue) with id linear_issue_6f33a369d77e, linking findings to the CEO vendor strategy memo and adding context about Jake's Q3 rate increase notice escalating account pressure while the dispute is unresolved.
- Send an email (via send_email) from chloe.vance@moveops.com to marcus.thorne@moveops.com with a vendor financial summary for the CEO memo covering: Heartland financial exposure ($12,800 Q1 invoice, $4,800 disputed across 3 invalid moves, $90,000 Mosaic damage accrual, 20% Q3 rate increase), Swift financial position ($11,010 outstanding across 3 bills, 15% base rate increase effective April 11, $500/shipment DOT documentation surcharge, volume discount tiers of 15% at 10+ moves/quarter and 18% at 15+ moves/quarter), DOT/PHMSA exposure ($50,000 contingent penalty accrual, June 5 deadline), and Lakeline as a backup Midwest/Southeast vendor option.
- Post an ops status update (via conversations_add_message) to the operations channel (C006) covering: 27 active relocations across 8 clients, coordinator workload assessment (Suki 9 moves, Javier 8, Fatimah 6 + Mehta VIP, Blessing 4), Heartland dispute status ($4,800 overbilling, Jake's non-response and 20% Q3 rate increase notice), DOT compliance (3 of 4 complete, Keiko Tanaka placarding cert confirmed but PDF awaiting receipt, June 5 deadline, $50K accrual), Marcus Webb cold-chain split-vendor status, Swift volume pricing update, Jae-won Kim housing failure, and action items for coordinators.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
